summary: Phase-2 EXPERT MODE DIAGNOSTICS (diagnostics-only backend change) verified end-to-end via /api/phonemes/analyze-formants. Extended /app/backend/tests/test_phoneme_formants_phase2.py from 35 → 45 tests (all passing, ~40s wall time). Added 4 new test classes covering every review-request requirement: (1) TestExpertDiagnosticsInResponse — a plausible /ʊ/ WAV yields 200 with a top-level result.diagnostics dict containing max_num_formants==5, ceiling_range_tested_hz==[5500,5000,4500], ceiling_selected_hz in {5500,5000,4500}, non-empty candidate_formants (each with start_ms/end_ms/F1/F2/F3/sd_f1f2/plausible+plausible is bool), nucleus_window_ms.start/.end numeric, reliable==True, and attempts list of per-ceiling dicts; and the diagnostics key does NOT leak into student_formants (student_formants has ONLY F1/F2/F3/F0/reliable). (2) TestExpertDiagnosticsStructuredLog — after a reliable POST the backend log /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log contains an 'analyze-formants[expert]:' line containing ceiling_selected_hz, candidate_formants, nucleus_window_ms and 'reliable': True; after an unreliable (F1~1250) POST (phoneme_ipa=æ) the endpoint returns 422 AND still emits 'analyze-formants[expert]:' with 'reliable': False + the three diagnostic markers so the oscillation can be studied from logs. (3) TestExpertDiagnosticsNucleusWindow — nucleus_window_ms.start and .end are numeric ms with start<end, and match exactly the start_ms/end_ms of the FIRST plausible entry in candidate_formants (chosen most-stable window). (4) TestExpertDiagnosticsNoScoringRegression — normal /ʊ/ still returns reference_source='dataset', composite_score, cefr.band, per_formant references == Hillenbrand dataset means for the F0-selected group; _weighted_composite([{'F1':0},{'F2':59},{'F3':88}]) still ≈62.5; word target on /ʊ/ still uses dataset; consent gate still 403 without audio consent (then re-granted for teardown). Regression coverage untouched: all 35 pre-existing tests continue to pass (BUG-1..4 fixes, F0 fixed thresholds 120→men/200→women/280→children, silent-fallback→422+WARNING logs, 5×-identical-POST reproducibility, retry ceilings 5500/5000/4500, etc.). Manually spot-checked a raw /ʊ/ POST → result.diagnostics.candidate_formants has 6 windows and diagnostics is present at top-level, absent from student_formants.
critical_code_review_comments: phoneme_formants.py — the expert log line is emitted via logging.info/logging.warning('analyze-formants[expert]: %s', diag_dict). The dict is stringified via its repr which is deterministic in CPython 3.7+ (insertion-ordered) and human-readable, but if any log aggregator downstream tries to parse it as JSON it will fail (single quotes, non-JSON booleans). Consider json.dumps(diag_dict) for the log line to make it grep-parseable in log-aggregation tools like Loki/ELK.

phoneme_formants.py:_extract_formants — the plausible-branch pops _diag before assigning _diagnostics, and the unreliable-branch also pops _diag; but both cases keep the diagnostics dict on the returned formants dict as '_diagnostics'. This is then popped again in analyze_formants (student.pop('_diagnostics', None)) and re-attached at result['diagnostics']. The double-pop dance works but is fragile — a future refactor that returns student_formants directly would leak the underscore key. Consider returning diagnostics as a SEPARATE return value from _extract_formants (tuple) so it never lives on the student_formants dict at all.

diagnostics.candidate_formants is capped at top-6 windows (diag_candidates = candidates[:6]). For the oscillation study the reviewer described (6 identical /æ/ recordings F1=573–897 & F3=2160–3273), 6 is sensible but consider making it configurable via env var (EXPERT_MAX_CANDIDATES) so field ops can dial it up temporarily during a debugging session.

attempts contains one entry PER ceiling but the plausible-return path breaks the loop at the first plausible result — so attempts for the plausible case may contain fewer than 3 entries (e.g. only 1 if 5500 Hz was plausible immediately). The test accepts this. Consider always iterating all 3 ceilings and only THEN selecting the plausible one — this gives operators a complete per-ceiling side-by-side comparison, which is exactly what the oscillation study needs (currently they see only the FIRST plausible ceiling, not what 5000/4500 would have produced for that same recording).

The expert log line for a reliable analysis is logged at INFO level; for unreliable at WARNING. This is documented in the endpoint file docstring implicitly ('INFO for reliable, WARNING for unreliable') but a leading '# noqa: G004' comment or explicit constant like LOG_EXPERT_INFO / LOG_EXPERT_WARN would make the intent clearer to future maintainers.
seed_data_creation: None — reused existing formant_references seeds (AmE men/women/children; RP male/female) and the pre-seeded teacher u-foot mp3 sample. Reused implausible_wav (F1~1250) and normal_u_wav (F1~430) module-scoped fixtures from iteration 36.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_phoneme_formants_phase2.py now has 45 tests (was 35). New EXPERT-mode diagnostics classes appended at end of file: TestExpertDiagnosticsInResponse (2 tests — full diagnostics structure + no-leak into student_formants), TestExpertDiagnosticsStructuredLog (2 tests — 'analyze-formants[expert]:' log line for both reliable-INFO and unreliable-WARNING cases), TestExpertDiagnosticsNucleusWindow (2 tests — start<end numeric ms + matches first-plausible candidate window), TestExpertDiagnosticsNoScoringRegression (4 tests — dataset scoring, _weighted_composite=62.5, word-target uses dataset, consent gate still 403). No new fixtures introduced. To run only the new tests: cd /app/backend && REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://canonical-voice-lab.preview.emergentagent.com python -m pytest tests/test_phoneme_formants_phase2.py -v -k 'Expert'. To run all: drop the -k filter (45 tests, ~40s).
rca of the issue: N/A — iteration 37 is a diagnostics-only extension (no bug being fixed). Main agent added the diagnostics dict + expert log line so operators can study why the FormantPath tracker oscillates on identical /æ/ recordings (F1 573→897 Hz, F3 2160→3273 Hz across 6 identical takes). This iteration verifies via automated tests that (a) result.diagnostics is populated with the full FormantPath internals for every 200 response; (b) the 'analyze-formants[expert]:' structured log line is emitted for BOTH reliable and unreliable analyses (WARNING on unreliable so the oscillation regime is captured even when the endpoint 422s); (c) diagnostics does not leak into student_formants; (d) no scoring/reference behaviour changed.
